title: hieracium levicaule subsp. subfissicladum
intro: Hieracium levicaule subsp. subfissicladum, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to certain regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin issues, and infections. The health benefits of Hieracium levicaule subsp. subfissicladum include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant may include infusions, decoctions, or salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
